jokes.text: (SINGING) Time to wizz in a foaming bowl of china
jokes.setup: Homer humming Ring of Fire, building to bathroom urgency
jokes.punchline: Crude reimagining of Johnny Cash lyrics about urination
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer crude wordplay - perfectly in character but relies on bathroom humor shortcut

jokes.text: Son, can I have cuts? No! Back cuts? Okay. Sweet. Hey!
jokes.setup: Homer asks for cuts in bathroom line
jokes.punchline: Bart's logic: front cuts bad, back cuts somehow acceptable
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and character work - Bart's kid logic is perfectly absurd yet makes weird sense

jokes.text: Oh, God! It's baby blood!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to help with locked bathroom situation
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes the worst in classic Homer panic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's tendency to catastrophize

jokes.text: I tried the coat hanger again. I don't understand why we only try ideas once.
jokes.setup: Family asks how he solved the problem
jokes.punchline: Homer's weird logic about persistence vs. trying new solutions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - sounds almost philosophical but is completely backwards thinking

jokes.text: Dr. Hibbert? Is someone seriously ill? Oh, I wish! (LAUGHS)
jokes.setup: Homer asking if someone's sick when doctor arrives
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's inappropriate enthusiasm for illness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Hibbert character moment - his inappropriate cheerfulness about medical problems

jokes.text: Santa's Little Helper paid a bootie call to my purebred poodle, Rosa Barks
jokes.setup: Explaining the dog breeding situation
jokes.punchline: 'Rosa Barks' as poodle name pun on Rosa Parks
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but feels like standard Simpsons reference humor

jokes.text: And he had his eyes on her prize. I'm gonna drop that analogy now.
jokes.setup: Continuing euphemistic description of dog mating
jokes.punchline: Self-aware abandonment of increasingly inappropriate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy - character acknowledging their own inappropriate analogy

jokes.text: You just lost a box, pal!
jokes.setup: Homer seems pleased about getting puppies
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's oddly threatening response about losing a box
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing non-sequitur but not particularly strong

jokes.text: on our way to the clinic, I decided to give him a night his wang would never forget
jokes.setup: Explaining why the dog wasn't neutered
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but oddly touching justification
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crude sentiment mixed with unexpected sweetness

jokes.text: Wanna play some air hockey? Three hundred and fifty dollars an hour. You're on!
jokes.setup: At strip club, someone suggests air hockey
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately agrees despite outrageous price
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's impulsivity and poor judgment

jokes.text: You broke our deal! We had an oral contract!
jokes.setup: Marge angry about the unneutered dog
jokes.punchline: Marge using legalistic language for domestic dispute
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Marge being more serious than situation warrants

jokes.text: He's an irritable, walleyed, misfit, bastard! Just like Willie.
jokes.setup: Willie describing a puppy's negative traits
jokes.punchline: Willie realizing he's described himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie moment - perfect self-awareness delivered in his characteristic style

jokes.text: Hey, mister, would you like a puppy? No. But I'll take one!
jokes.setup: Bart asking if someone wants a puppy
jokes.punchline: Semantic distinction between wanting and taking
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good economy, though character integration is weak

jokes.text: Seen it... Bad... Boring... Saw it on the plane... Rerun... Rerun... Telemundo... Me... Turned it down... ABC... Banned from the set... Lame... Lame... Regis...
jokes.setup: Krusty channel surfing
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire dismissals revealing his entertainment industry history
jokes.explanation: Excellent character establishment - shows Krusty's industry experience through rapid commentary

jokes.text: Let's see, champagne or Slim Fast?
jokes.setup: Krusty contemplating his choices
jokes.punchline: Absurd pairing of celebration drink with diet drink
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Krusty's lifestyle contradictions

jokes.text: DSL! DSL! Who will buy my high-speed connections?
jokes.setup: Street vendors in old Jewish neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Modern technology being sold like old-world goods
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of old and new, though lacks character specificity

jokes.text: Brazilian wax! Get your velvety smooth, Brazilian wax!
jokes.setup: Following the DSL vendor joke
jokes.punchline: Even more inappropriate modern service being hawked traditionally
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke but relies more on crude shock value

jokes.text: Here, go on Sandy Koufax. I lost ten grand when he wouldn't pitch on Yom Kippur! I did five shows that night!
jokes.setup: Krusty looking at Jewish Walk of Fame
jokes.punchline: Krusty angry at Koufax for religious observance while he worked
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Krusty's complicated relationship with Jewish identity

jokes.text: Why ain't I here? I'm a bigger name than... Chaim Potok! What is he? Some kind of Klingon?
jokes.setup: Krusty wondering why he's not on the Walk of Fame
jokes.punchline: Dismissing famous Jewish author as alien species
jokes.explanation: Good character ignorance moment with solid pop culture mashup

jokes.text: Circumcision? And then some! (CHUCKLES)
jokes.setup: Rabbi asking about circumcision
jokes.punchline: Krusty implying extensive surgery/mutilation
jokes.explanation: Typical Krusty crude humor but relies on shock value shortcut

jokes.text: Do you know how much I donate to the B'nai B'rith? Actually, I do. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Krusty asking if rabbi knows his donation amount
jokes.punchline: Rabbi's matter-of-fact knowledge and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with implication that Krusty doesn't donate much

jokes.text: Oh, I was turned down by all those country clubs for nothing!
jokes.setup: Krusty learning he's not Jewish
jokes.punchline: Realizing anti-Semitism wasn't the reason for rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark irony - Krusty realizing his rejections were for other reasons entirely

jokes.text: I thought I was a self-hating Jew! But it turns out, I'm just a plain old anti-Semite!
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining his identity crisis
jokes.punchline: Horrifying realization about his actual prejudices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - takes Krusty's character to its logical extreme with perfect structure and timing

jokes.text: Without a Bar Mitzvah, I'm just a boy with a prostate the size of a goat's head!
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining importance of Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Grotesque medical imagery for comedic effect
jokes.explanation: Typical Krusty gross-out humor with memorable imagery

jokes.text: I remember learning to ride a bike, then pretty much nothing till right now.
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining memory gaps from hard living
jokes.punchline: Vast blank space of decades
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - suggests decades of substance abuse

jokes.text: Maybe I am but you can't argue with the laughs.
jokes.setup: Rabbi saying Krusty makes mockery of everything
jokes.punchline: Krusty's defense is purely pragmatic
jokes.explanation: Good character philosophy moment - Krusty's entertainment-above-all worldview

jokes.text: So what? It's not my job to make kids laugh. Yes, it is. You're a clown.
jokes.setup: Rabbi dismissing making kids laugh
jokes.punchline: Krusty pointing out the obvious about clown duties
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man setup with deadpan reality check

jokes.text: That'd be great. Are you sure that's kosher?
jokes.setup: Discussion about adult Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Using 'kosher' both literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Basic pun on kosher - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I have a Jewish imaginary friend. Her name is Rachel Cohen and she just got into Brandeis.
jokes.setup: How Lisa knows about Jewish customs
jokes.punchline: Highly specific imaginary friend with academic achievements
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa character moment - intellectually specific imaginary friend fits perfectly

jokes.text: Mouse-l Tov!
jokes.setup: Krusty's animated Jewish education segment
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Mazel Tov' with cartoon mouse
jokes.explanation: Standard cartoon pun - serviceable but not particularly clever

jokes.text: He's a son, a lover and a pundit rolled into one sexy package!
jokes.setup: Krusty praising Jon Stewart as guest host option
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate description ending in attraction
jokes.explanation: Krusty's inappropriate attraction disguised as professional admiration

jokes.text: Such expensive flowers, filled with remorse.
jokes.setup: Krusty talking about smelling flowers on Friday
jokes.punchline: Obvious drug reference disguised as poetic language
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic language for Krusty's drug use with nice poetic touch

jokes.text: Man, are you unpleasant! You're in!
jokes.setup: Krusty looking for replacement host
jokes.punchline: Homer's unpleasantness is exactly what's needed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's practical cynicism about television

jokes.text: Welcome to The Homer Simpson Show. I'm your host, next card. Homer Simpson
jokes.setup: Homer doing TV host introduction
jokes.punchline: Reading cue cards literally, including instructions
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence - perfect character moment of missing obvious cues

jokes.text: Carl Carlson, raconteur, and Lenny Leonard, three-time juror
jokes.setup: Homer introducing his guests
jokes.punchline: Absurdly mundane credentials, especially jury duty
jokes.explanation: Great absurd credentials - jury duty as career highlight is perfectly Homer-logic

jokes.text: You ever notice how seats are too small for normal looking guys like me? Theaters, airplanes? Why even here! Look!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be a talk show host
jokes.punchline: Calling himself 'normal looking' while clearly overweight
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke territory but good Homer self-delusion

jokes.text: Never go on the teacup ride after eating at Beauty and the Beast's Fried Dough Chateau.
jokes.setup: Carl contributing to talk show discussion
jokes.punchline: Overly specific Disney theme park advice
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor with good specificity

jokes.text: How about some new oldies, geniuses?
jokes.setup: Lenny complaining about radio stations
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic concept of 'new oldies'
jokes.explanation: Good paradoxical concept with economic delivery

jokes.text: Who has more power? Miss America or Miss USA? I think one's elected and the other one's appointed.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about beauty pageants
jokes.punchline: Homer treating pageants like government positions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - applying political structure to beauty pageants

jokes.text: Go home, turn on your television, Channel Six. Thanks for the tip!
jokes.setup: Prostitute propositioning Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding and giving TV recommendation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness moment with nice misdirection

jokes.text: I think one's elected and the other one's appointed.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering about Miss America vs Miss USA power
jokes.punchline: Treating beauty pageants like government positions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - applying political framework to beauty contests

jokes.text: All these rules! I feel like I'm in a strip club!
jokes.setup: Rabbi explaining kosher laws
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing religious restrictions to strip club rules
jokes.explanation: Good callback to strip club with inappropriate comparison to religious law

jokes.text: All these rules! I feel like I'm in a strip club!
jokes.setup: Rabbi explaining kosher laws to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing religious rules to strip club restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier scene with perfect Homer inappropriate comparison

jokes.text: I was wondering if I could get a small cost of living raise? Interesting. Please welcome new panelist, Barney Gumble.
jokes.setup: Lenny asking for raise on air
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately replacing him instead of answering
jokes.explanation: Excellent power-mad Homer moment - ruthless firing disguised as TV hosting

jokes.text: That light's a little bright. You think you can move it? Please welcome our newest panelist, Disco Stu.
jokes.setup: Barney making simple request about lighting
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately firing him too for minor complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's power abuse - firing for even smaller infractions

jokes.text: Disco Stu knows his place.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu being brought in as replacement panelist
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acceptance of being disposable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Disco Stu character moment - third-person self-awareness with resignation

jokes.text: My husband's a power-mad star. Maybe now we can get that padding that goes under rugs.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Homer's TV success
jokes.punchline: Mundane domestic priorities despite celebrity status
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge character moment - practical concerns amid Hollywood glamour

jokes.text: You're not doing this fast enough! You're fired, too!
jokes.setup: Executive firing Krusty
jokes.punchline: Executive firing himself for being too slow at firing
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - executive's impatience leads to self-firing

jokes.text: I eat Legos. So did I, son. And look where I am today.
jokes.setup: Child admitting to eating toys
jokes.punchline: Homer treating Lego consumption as path to success
jokes.explanation: Good Homer wisdom - finding causation in random childhood behavior

jokes.text: I eat Legos. So did I, son. And look where I am today.
jokes.setup: Kid making random statement while getting autograph
jokes.punchline: Homer treating childhood stupidity as career advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer wisdom moment - connecting childhood behavior to adult success illogically

jokes.text: Duck, Mister Lincoln!
jokes.setup: Homer using his TV power for good
jokes.punchline: Warning Lincoln about assassination via television
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept with Homer's well-meaning stupidity

jokes.text: I say live fast, die young, and leave a big fat corpse!
jokes.setup: Homer being asked to say meaningful things
jokes.punchline: Corrupting famous quote to match his physique
jokes.explanation: Good twist on famous quote that perfectly fits Homer's character

jokes.text: How about a show where girls think I'm a millionaire? But what they don't know is I'm rife with disease!
jokes.setup: Krusty pitching reality TV shows
jokes.punchline: Horrifying twist on dating show concept
jokes.explanation: Dark but creative reality TV satire with perfect Krusty desperation

jokes.text: Please, you people are known for taking chances on crap.
jokes.setup: Krusty pitching to Fox executives
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of Fox programming standards
jokes.explanation: Good self-referential Fox joke with economic delivery

jokes.text: 'Boobs.' (LAUGHS) Just like what girls got.
jokes.setup: Discussion about calculator spelling
jokes.punchline: Homer's juvenile explanation of the word
jokes.explanation: Very basic humor but perfectly captures Homer's juvenile mindset

jokes.text: Yet the IMF insists on unrealistic debt repayment schedules.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to discuss serious issues per Lisa's advice
jokes.punchline: Homer unexpectedly knowledgeable about international finance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - Homer displaying unexpected intelligence

jokes.text: This show stinks! I knew my son would blow it! Where's the remote? Oh, it might as well be in China.
jokes.setup: Grampa watching Homer's meaningful show
jokes.punchline: Escalating from criticism to geographical hyperbole about remote
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa character moment with typical curmudgeonly escalation

jokes.text: T, I haven't seen you since we roasted Chevy Chase! Yeah. We were kind of rough on him. I feel bad for the guy. You pitied the fool? That's one way of putting it.
jokes.setup: Krusty and Mr. T reunion conversation
jokes.punchline: Playing on Mr. T's catchphrase in context of regret
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity catchphrase integration with mild emotional twist

jokes.text: Baruch adonai hamvorach, fool!
jokes.setup: Mr. T participating in Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Hebrew blessing combined with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Creative cultural mashup that maintains both character integrity and religious context

jokes.text: So that's why Bart has all those broken tracheal bones. Tight bow tie, my ass!
jokes.setup: Homer choking Bart
jokes.punchline: Marge's realization about Bart's medical condition and Homer's excuse
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant callback to Homer's strangling habit with medical consequences

jokes.text: That's a lot of latke!
jokes.setup: Giant potato pancake at Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'That's a lot of [something]' with Jewish food
jokes.explanation: Basic pun with Jewish food - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Marge, I thank God every day you settled for me.
jokes.setup: Marge praising Homer's classy exit
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware acknowledgment of being a compromise
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer self-awareness delivered as romantic gratitude

jokes.text: Marge, I thank God every day you settled for me.
jokes.setup: Marge complimenting Homer on his classy exit
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware gratitude for Marge's low standards
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment with perfect self-awareness about their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: I pity the shul that won't let Krusty in now!
jokes.setup: Mr. T at Krusty's Bar Mitzvah finale
jokes.punchline: His catchphrase adapted for synagogue context
jokes.explanation: Good integration of catchphrase with Jewish terminology

jokes.text: I wish I had invested my money better.
jokes.setup: Krusty's successful Bar Mitzvah celebration
jokes.punchline: Moment of financial regret during triumph
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's perpetual financial anxiety

jokes.text: Krusty, the ratings were good. Raymond re-run good.
jokes.setup: Executive reporting Bar Mitzvah TV ratings
jokes.punchline: Comparing to specific sitcom rerun success
jokes.explanation: Good industry reference - specific TV success metric

jokes.text: I'm hungry. Are they married yet? They're not getting married. Then whose garter is this?
jokes.setup: Homer confused during Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong about ceremony type and mysterious garter
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion with mysterious visual element

jokes.text: I pity the fool who didn't bring an envelope to this Bar Mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Mr. T at end of Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Catchphrase applied to Jewish gift-giving tradition
jokes.explanation: Another solid Mr. T catchphrase integration with Jewish customs

jokes.text: I pity the fool who didn't bring an envelope to this Bar Mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Mr. T closing the Bar Mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Catchphrase about Jewish gift-giving tradition
jokes.explanation: Final Mr. T catchphrase with Jewish tradition reference, solid closing
